fields.name: Houston to Atlanta LTL
fields.seo-title: Houston, TX to Atlanta, GA LTL Freight | WARP
fields.seo-meta-description: Ship LTL freight from Houston to Atlanta: 827-mile lane, 3–4 day transit, 10 carriers. Get instant rates and real-time tracking through WARP.
fields.hero-headline: Houston, TX to Atlanta, GA LTL Freight
fields.subheadline: 827-mile LTL corridor with 3–4 day transit. 10 vetted carriers providing Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled) capacity, real-time tracking, and managed operations.
fields.body-content:
LTL freight from Houston, TX to Atlanta, GA covers 827 miles across the South Central to Southeast corridor. Standard transit on this interstate lane runs 3–4 business days. WARP covers this corridor with 10 vetted carriers and Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled) capacity, providing pallet-level tracking and managed appointment scheduling on every shipment.

WARP's LTL service on this lane includes pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery, managed appointment scheduling, and delivery confirmation handled by WARP's operations team. Every shipment receives real-time scan events at pickup, each in-transit checkpoint, and final delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change — delayed pickups, missed scans, or appointment changes are flagged and escalated before they become problems. Carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution happen without requiring shipper follow-up.

Cross-dock facilities at Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub, Memphis Freight Hub support consolidation and re-routing on this corridor. When exceptions occur — weather delays, equipment failures, or volume surges — this infrastructure enables alternate routing without starting the shipment process over. Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

LTL rates on the Houston to Atlanta lane typically range from $2,937 to $3,916 depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and accessorials. Key cost drivers include NMFC classification, shipment density, and services like liftgate or inside delivery. At 827 miles, the per-mile linehaul component averages approximately $4.14/mile. Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item. These are modeled estimates — enter your shipment details at wearewarp.com/quote for a real-time, all-in rate.

This lane is a strong fit for 1–6 pallet shipments with standard or appointment-based delivery requirements, recurring retail or distribution replenishment freight, and any LTL load where real-time tracking and proactive exception management matter more than finding the lowest one-time rate. Shippers consolidating carrier relationships to reduce vendor complexity see the most value on corridors like Houston to Atlanta.

Get an instant LTL rate for the Houston to Atlanta lane at wearewarp.com/quote. Compare carrier options, book with a single click, and track every shipment with real-time milestone updates. No commitment required. See detailed operating data, pricing factors, and frequently asked questions for this corridor below.

Freight Class Pricing: Opaque, accessorials added after quote
Damage Risk: Limited visibility until claim filed

Why the Houston to Atlanta Corridor Matters

The Houston to Atlanta LTL lane is a freight corridor connecting the South Central and Southeast regions. This 827-mile interstate lane connects two distinct freight markets, with transit driven by linehaul routing and terminal schedules. Standard LTL transit runs 3–4 business days. WARP operates this lane with 10 vetted carriers and common equipment including Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub, Memphis Freight Hub support consolidation and transit optimization for this corridor. Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

  • ▸ 827-mile corridor connecting South Central and Southeast regions
  • ▸ 3–4 business day standard transit for LTL service
  • ▸ 10 vetted carriers with Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled) capacity
  • ▸ Cross-dock access at Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub, Memphis Freight Hub
  • ▸ Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

LTL Pricing Factors: Houston to Atlanta

LTL rates on the Houston to Atlanta lane typically range from $2,937 to $3,916, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand. Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup. At 827 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $4.14/mile). Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices. These are modeled estimates. Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Best-Fit Shipments: Houston to Atlanta LTL

WARP is a strong fit for LTL shipments on the Houston to Atlanta lane when you need predictable transit, real-time visibility, and operational support without managing carrier relationships directly.

  • ▸ 1–6 pallet shipments with standard or appointment-based delivery requirements
  • ▸ Recurring retail or distribution replenishment freight on this corridor
  • ▸ Shipments requiring real-time tracking and proactive exception management
  • ▸ Freight that needs transparent, all-in pricing without manual quote follow-up
  • ▸ Shippers consolidating carrier relationships to reduce vendor complexity
  • ▸ Freight benefiting from cross-dock consolidation at Houston Terminal or Dallas Mega Hub

Cross-dock facilities at Houston Terminal and Dallas Mega Hub support consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur on this corridor. This infrastructure enables better transit reliability on the Houston to Atlanta lane.

Frequently Asked Questions: Houston to Atlanta LTL

How much does LTL freight from Houston, TX to Atlanta, GA cost?

Estimated LTL rates on this lane range from $2,937 to $3,916, based on freight class, weight, pallet count, and current market conditions. Enter your shipment details on WARP for an instant, all-in rate.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Houston to Atlanta?

Standard LTL transit on the Houston to Atlanta lane is 3–4 business days. This 827-mile corridor's actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Houston to Atlanta lane?

WARP operates this lane with approximately 10 vetted carriers. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and on-time performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Houston to Atlanta in real time?

Yes. Every WARP shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly without requiring shipper follow-up.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Houston to Atlanta corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.

What affects LTL freight rates from Houston to Atlanta?

Key rate factors on this lane include freight class (NMFC classification), shipment weight and density, pallet count, accessorial services (liftgate, inside delivery, limited access), seasonal demand, and current carrier capacity. Fuel surcharges are applied separately based on current diesel prices.

What makes WARP different from traditional LTL freight brokers?

WARP provides self-serve instant quoting (under 2 minutes vs. hours with traditional brokers), a side-by-side carrier comparison dashboard, one-click booking with digital BOL, real-time tracking with proactive exception alerts, and managed operations where WARP handles carrier coordination and resolution directly.
